course_description:
Recognition, prevention & management of injuries from physical activity; mechanisms of sports injuries; preventive & protective techniques; special conditions in sports medicine. This course had the previous code of HMST3052 which can be viewed in the archived area within the Course and Program site http://www.uq.edu.au/study/archive/index.html

SPMD3052 is concerned with the total health care of exercising individuals and athletes based on the best available scientific and clinical evidence.

SPMD3052 focuses on the field of sports and exercise medicine from a broad perspective relevant toﾠstudents human movement and nutrition sciences. ﾠ

SPMD3052 incorporates team teaching , in line with the interdisciplinary nature of sports and exercise medicine, with online lectures and workshops developed by the course coordinator and invited presenters from various academic and clinical backgrounds.

The MINIMUM expected workload for SPMD3052 is 10 hours per week involving studying a series of weekly online lectures, aligned textbook readings (Brukner & Khan) and "workshop" sessions as timetabled.

course_aims: The aims of SPMD3052 are to facilitate you to:

learning_outcomes.description: demonstrate a detailed understanding of acute and overuse injuries and injury prevention and evidence based principles in the field of sports and exercise medicine
develop, evaluate and apply knowledge and evidence based information regarding bone and soft-tissue injuries of the shoulder joint
develop, evaluate and apply knowledge and evidence based information regarding bone and soft-tissue injuries of the elbow joint
develop, evaluate and apply knowledge and evidence based information regarding bone and soft-tissue injuries of the wrist joint, hand and fingers
develop, evaluate and apply knowledge and evidence based information regarding bone and soft-tissue injuries of the hip joint and thigh
develop, evaluate and apply knowledge and evidence based information regarding bone and soft-tissue injuries of the knee joint
develop, evaluate and apply knowledge and evidence based information regarding bone and soft-tissue injuries of the ankle joint
develop, evaluate and apply knowledge and evidence based information regarding major chronic conditions in sports and exercise medicine
develop, evaluate and apply knowledge and evidence based information regarding specific athlete populations in sports and exercise medicine

assessment_details.title: Workshop Case Studies
assessment_details.due_date: Workshop 1 24/02/2026 Workshop 2 3/03/2026 Workshop 3 10/03/2026 Workshop 4 17/03/2026 Workshop 5 31/03/2026 Workshop 6 14/04/2026 Workshop 7 21/04/2026 Workshop 8 5/05/2026 Workshop 9 12/05/2026 Workshop 10 19/05/2026 During timetabled workshop.
assessment_details.task_description: During scheduled workshop sessions, students will complete case study task(s) designed to apply and integrate key concepts from online lectures and course materials. Workshops require attendance and active participation. Students are expected to engage in discussion, collaborate where required, and contribute meaningfully to the analysis and resolution of the case study task(s) during the timetabled session. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Students are permitted to miss zero workshops for SPMD3052 without receiving approval as there is assessment completed in the workshop sessions.
Please follow the process for absences (below) as soon as you possibly know that you will be absent from a practical. The earlier we are made aware, the easier it is to make alternative arrangements.
If you are unable to attend for medical or extenuating circumstances , you are required to complete an absentee form and email the form as soon as possible to the School of Human Movement and Nutrition Sciences Student Administration Team ( hmns@enquire.uq.edu.au ) and no later than two (2) calendar days after the date of the original class was held.
With approval, you will be permitted to attend a make-up session as arranged in consultation with the Course Coordinator.
Failure to attend a make-up session or non-approved absences will result in a mark of zero (0) for the associated workshop task.
Late submission
You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .
